**Operators** are software extensions to: **Operators** are software extensions to OpenShift that make use of custom resources to manage applications and their components. They are managed by Operator Lifecycle Manager.

By disabling a default source, the opera: By disabling a default source, the operators it provides will no longer appear in Software Catalog and any operator that has been installed from this source will no longer receive updates until the source is re-enabled. Disabling the source will also remove the corresponding OperatorSource and CatalogSource resources from the cluster.

Community Operators are Operators which: Community Operators are Operators which have not been vetted or verified by Red Hat. Community Operators should be used with caution because their stability is unknown. Red Hat provides no support for community Operators.

Install your Operator by subscribing to: Install your Operator by subscribing to one of the update channels to keep the Operator up to date. The strategy determines either manual or automatic updates.
InstallPlans are created automatically b: InstallPlans are created automatically by subscriptions or manually using the CLI.

Please note that installing non-Red Hat: Please note that installing non-Red Hat operators into OpenShift namespaces and enabling monitoring voids user support. Enabling cluster monitoring for non-Red Hat operators can lead to malicious metrics data overriding existing cluster metrics.

The Operator does not support to be made: The Operator does not support to be made available in a single namespace (OwnNamespace installMode) or global installation (AllNamespaces installMode).  Use the CLI to install this Operator instead.

The {{namespace}} Namespace is reserved: The {{namespace}} Namespace is reserved for global Operators that watch all Namespaces. To install an Operator in a single Namespace, select a different Namespace where the operand should run.

This cluster is using AWS Security Token: This cluster is using AWS Security Token Service to reach the cloud API. In order for this operator to take the actions it requires directly with the cloud API, you must provide a role ARN (with an attached policy) during installation. Please see the operator description for more details.

This cluster is using AWS Security Token Service to reach the cloud API. In order for this operator to take the actions it requires directly with the cloud API, you will need to provide a role ARN (with an attached policy) during installation. Manual subscriptions are highly recommended as steps should be taken prior to upgrade to ensure that the permissions required by the next version are properly accounted for in the role. Please see the operator description for more details.
This cluster is using Azure Workload Ide: This cluster is using Azure Workload Identity / Federated Identity to reach the cloud API. In order for this operator to take the actions it requires directly with the cloud API, provide the Client ID, Tenant ID, and Subscription ID during installation. Manual subscriptions are highly recommended as steps should be taken before upgrade to ensure that the permissions required by the next version are properly accounted for in the role. See the operator description for more details.

Manual subscriptions are highly recommended as steps should be taken before upgrade to ensure that the permissions required by the next version are properly accounted for in the role. This console plugin will be able to prov: This console plugin will be able to provide a custom interface and run any Kubernetes command as the logged in user. Make sure you trust it before enabling.
This is a community provided Operator. T: This is a community provided Operator. These are Operators which have not been vetted or verified by Red Hat. Community Operators should be used with caution because their stability is unknown. Red Hat provides no support for community Operators.

This operator includes a console plugin: This operator includes a console plugin which provides a custom interface that can be included in the console. The console plugin will prompt for the console to be refreshed once it has been enabled. Make sure you trust this console plugin before enabling.
This resource is provided by {{operatorN: This resource is provided by {{operatorName}}, a Kubernetes Operator enabled by the Operator Lifecycle Manager.
